name: Seated Dumbbell Curl
resistance_profile: Hardest at the midpoint with the forearm horizontal, where the dumbbell's moment arm is longest; tension eases at the bottom and the top.
cues: Curl without swinging the elbows forward
Squeeze at the top, control down
execution_steps: Sit upright on a bench with a dumbbell in each hand, arms hanging at your sides.
Keep your elbows pinned near your torso and your chest up.
Curl the dumbbells up toward your shoulders, supinating as you lift.
Squeeze at the top, then lower under control to a full extension.
good_when: You want a stricter curl than standing because the seated position limits swinging.
You want to train each arm independently to fix imbalances (alternate arms).
bad_when: You want a stretch-position emphasis (use an incline curl).
You want to move maximal load bilaterally (use a barbell curl).
